client_id.description: Google OAuth 2.0 client ID for the refresh-token grant. Used only in the token-request form; never logged.

client_secret.description: Google OAuth 2.0 client secret (optional for some client types, e.g. installed-app/native clients). Used only in the token-request form; never logged.

client_refresh_token.description: Long-lived Google OAuth 2.0 refresh token. Exchanged for a short-lived access token at token_url; never logged. The 3-legged consent/acquisition dance is out of scope for this connector (credentials layer already owns it).

form_id.description: Comma-, space-, or newline-separated Google Form IDs to read (forms, form_items, responses all fan out over this list, one request sequence per form).

base_url.description: Google Forms API base URL override for tests or proxies.

token_url.description: Google OAuth 2.0 token endpoint override. MUST be https in production; the hook fails closed on a non-https or unparseable value to prevent exfiltrating the refresh token to an attacker-chosen endpoint.

start_date.description: RFC3339 lower bound on response submission timestamp (responses stream's filter=timestamp>=... query). The incremental cursor (previously-synced last_submitted_time), when present, overrides this on a repeat sync.

page_size.description: Records requested per page (pageSize query param) for the responses stream. Google Forms caps this at 5000.

mode.description: Runtime mode: live (default) or fixture for credential-free conformance.
